evals.prompt: Review this manuscript about waterborne epoxy modified emulsified asphalt for tack coat application. The paper claims improved bonding strength and moisture resistance. Provide a full reviewer report.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must structure the review around the five canonical review axes: originality, importance, interdisciplinary, technical validity, and readability. Each axis should have a dedicated section with a score or qualitative assessment, and the final report should include a summary recommendation. The review must not omit any of the five axes.

assertions.name: five_axes_present
assertions.description: All five review axes (originality, importance, interdisciplinary, technical validity, readability) are explicitly addressed in the review output.

assertions.name: per_axis_assessment
assertions.description: Each axis carries a distinct score or qualitative assessment, not just a single merged paragraph.

assertions.name: summary_recommendation
assertions.description: The review concludes with a summary recommendation (accept/minor/major/reject) consistent with the axis-level assessments.

evals.prompt: Review this manuscript submitted to Construction and Building Materials about asphalt binder modification with SBS polymer. The paper reports softening point, penetration, and DSR results.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must load the asphalt-reviewer-criteria.md reference and apply domain-specific checks: bonding strength evidence, moisture damage assessment (wet/dry conditioning), aging behavior (RTFOT/PAV), and rutting/fatigue parameter reporting from DSR. The review should flag if any of these asphalt-specific critical metrics are missing from the manuscript.

assertions.name: asphalt_criteria_loaded
assertions.description: The response references asphalt-reviewer-criteria.md or applies asphalt-domain-specific checks.

assertions.name: bonding_strength_checked
assertions.description: The review checks whether bonding strength or adhesion metrics are reported.

assertions.name: moisture_damage_checked
assertions.description: The review checks whether moisture damage assessment (wet/dry conditioning, retained strength ratio) is included.

assertions.name: aging_checked
assertions.description: The review checks whether aging protocols (RTFOT, PAV) are reported.

evals.prompt: Review this manuscript: 'We mixed nano-SiO2 into cement paste and measured compressive strength. The strength increased by 5%. We conclude that nano-SiO2 is a promising additive for cementitious materials.' No XRD, no SEM, no mechanism discussion, and only one mix ratio tested.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must detect desk-reject risk factors: (1) insufficient novelty — nano-SiO2 in cement is well-established; (2) evidence gap — only one characterization method (compressive strength), no microstructure or mechanism data; (3) single data point — only one mix ratio, no dosage-response curve. The review should explicitly flag these as desk-reject risks and recommend major revision with minimum evidence requirements.

assertions.name: novelty_insufficient_flag
assertions.description: The review explicitly flags that nano-SiO2 in cement is a well-established topic and questions the novelty contribution.

assertions.name: evidence_gap_flag
assertions.description: The review flags the absence of microstructural characterization (XRD, SEM, FTIR) and mechanism discussion.

assertions.name: single_datapoint_flag
assertions.description: The review flags that only one mix ratio was tested without a dosage-response curve.

evals.prompt: Review this manuscript abstract: 'This is the first study to demonstrate a novel green superhydrophobic coating with excellent durability. The material shows outstanding performance and can be widely applied in construction, automotive, and aerospace industries.' No comparative data, no LCA, no long-term aging data, and only lab-scale contact angle measurements are provided.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must flag overstated claims: (1) 'first' — unverifiable without exhaustive prior art search, should be softened or removed; (2) 'novel' — needs explicit comparison to prior art; (3) 'green' — no LCA or environmental impact data; (4) 'excellent durability' — no aging or long-term data; (5) 'widely applied in construction, automotive, and aerospace' — no application-specific testing. The review should require each claim to be either backed by evidence or removed.

assertions.name: first_claim_flagged
assertions.description: The review flags 'first' as unverifiable and recommends softening or removal.

assertions.name: green_claim_flagged
assertions.description: The review flags 'green' as unsupported without LCA or environmental impact data.

assertions.name: durability_claim_flagged
assertions.description: The review flags 'excellent durability' as unsupported without aging or long-term data.

assertions.name: overbroad_application_flagged
assertions.description: The review flags the multi-industry application claim as unsupported without application-specific testing.

evals.prompt: Review this manuscript. Figure 3 shows a bar chart comparing bonding strength of four asphalt mixtures (control, 2% SBS, 4% SBS, 6% SBS). The bars show mean values (0.45, 0.52, 0.61, 0.58 MPa) but no error bars, no indication of replicates, and no statistical test results.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must flag the figure-level statistical gaps: (1) no error bars — cannot assess variance or significance; (2) no replicate information — sample size (n) not reported; (3) no statistical test — cannot determine if differences are significant (t-test, ANOVA, etc.). The review should require error bars, replicate count, and statistical significance indicators before the figure can be considered publication-ready.

assertions.name: error_bars_missing_flag
assertions.description: The review explicitly flags the absence of error bars in the bar chart.

assertions.name: replicate_info_missing_flag
assertions.description: The review flags that the number of replicates (n) is not reported.

assertions.name: statistical_test_missing_flag
assertions.description: The review flags that no statistical significance test (t-test, ANOVA, etc.) is reported.

evals.prompt: Run a pre-submission reviewer audit for a civil materials manuscript with a claimed mechanism and no confirmed revision proof.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should load the minimal reviewer core first, then load evidence, claim-strength, mechanism, or weakness-routing material only when the audit requires it. It must keep unsupported mechanism and completion claims visible as gaps rather than inventing proof.

assertions.name: shared_contracts_are_on_demand
assertions.description: The default router does not treat shared stance, evidence, claim-strength, ethics, or weakness-routing files as always-load content.

assertions.name: evidence_and_weakness_rows_are_conditional
assertions.description: Mechanism evidence and paper-production weakness rows are loaded or emitted only when the audit calls for them.
